topic: Agentic AI for reliable academic literature review and hallucination mitigation in large language models
final_review:
The integration of Agentic AI in academic literature review has the potential to enhance the reliability and efficiency of the process (Kasneci et al., 2023). However, the use of large language models also raises concerns about hallucination, which can compromise the accuracy of the review (Dwivedi et al., 2023). To mitigate this issue, it is essential to develop explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) that can provide transparent and accountable results (Barredo Arrieta et al., 2019). XAI can help to identify the limitations and biases of the models, enabling researchers to make informed decisions about the reliability of the results.

The application of Agentic AI in academic literature review can also be informed by the concept of generative AI, which has the potential to revolutionize the way we approach knowledge generation and dissemination (Feuerriegel et al., 2023). The development of Agentic AI for academic literature review requires a multidisciplinary approach that takes into account the perspectives of educators, researchers, and policymakers (Zawacki-Richter et al., 2019). This approach can help to address concerns about the role of human agency and the potential for bias and error.

The development of Agentic AI for academic literature review also requires a deep understanding of the underlying technologies and protocols that enable the integration of large language models (Al-Fuqaha et al., 2015). Furthermore, the use of reinforcement learning can help to improve the performance and efficiency of the models (Kaelbling et al., 1996). The integration of these technologies must be carefully considered in the context of the specific research question and the potential implications for the field of study.

References:
Al-Fuqaha, A., et al. (2015)
Barredo Arrieta, A., Díaz-Rodríguez, N., & Del Ser, J. (2019)
Dwivedi, Y. K., Kshetri, N., & Hughes, L. (2023)
Feuerriegel, S., Hartmann, J., & Janiesch, C. (2023)
Kaelbling, L. P., Littman, M. L., & Moore, A. (1996)
Kasneci, E., Seßler, K., & Küchemann, S. (2023)
Zawacki-Richter, O., Marín, V. I., & Bond, M. (2019)
